---
title: "Geog 510: GIS and Spatial Analysis for Public Health"
date: last-modified
date-format: medium
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: yes
    toc_float: yes
    toc_depth: 3
---

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Instructor Information

**Instructor:** Dr. Varun Goel

**Office:** Callcott 111

\
**Appointment link:** [Click Here](https://outlook.office.com/bookwithme/user/675339bb37584ebaa37d12437cb4f286@sc.edu/meetingtype/aKkUKwRTska6ndT42c8OWg2?anonymous&ep=mlink)

**Email:** <a href="mailto:varungoel@sc.edu">varungoel\@sc.edu</a>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Course Essentials

**Meeting times:** Tuesday, Thursday *2:50 pm - 4:05pm*\
**Meeting location:** Callcott 302

**Student Support Hours:** Tu-Th  1:30 pm -  2:30 pm

**Appointment link:** [Click Here](https://outlook.office.com/bookwithme/user/675339bb37584ebaa37d12437cb4f286@sc.edu/meetingtype/aKkUKwRTska6ndT42c8OWg2?anonymous&ep=mlink)


------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Required Text

Cromley, E. K., & McLaferty, S. L. (2012). GIS and Public Health (2nd edition). New York: Guilford Press. [**This is available for free via the USC Library!**](https://pascal-usc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/discovery/fulldisplay?docid=alma991027642162405618&context=L&vid=01PASCAL_USCCOL:USC&lang=en&search_scope=MyInst_and_CI&adaptor=Local%20Search%20Engine&tab=Everything&query=any,contains,gis%20and%20public%20health&mode=basic) I will also post .pdfs of the chapters on blackboard. Additional readings will be posted on blackboard or available online. See the Readings document for a list of all course readings.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Updated Syllabus

The latest class syllabus can downloaded [here](./Geog510_GISandHealth_SP26_013026.pdf)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Link to Class Discussion

Post any of your queries/questions/comments [here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1FEtZD2uAuDbaQHtuEyQqBXclLkJXjtFUODYfxlVX74U/edit?usp=sharing)


```
